name: How much is a typical settlement for a crushed foot or broken leg from a forklift accident?
description: Settlements are based on medical costs, lost wages, and your permanent impairment rating. Serious forklift injuries involving surgery or permanent restrictions

mainEntity.name: How much is a typical settlement for a crushed foot or broken leg from a forklift accident?

acceptedAnswer.text: Settlements are based on medical costs, lost wages, and your permanent impairment rating. Serious forklift injuries involving surgery or permanent restrictions often result in significant five- or six-figure settlements depending on your prior earnings.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Forklift accidents often result in high-value claims because the injuries—such as crushed limbs, spinal damage, or internal trauma—are frequently catastrophic. Your settlement value is primarily determined by your medical expenses, your "permanent partial disability" (PPD) rating once you reach maximum improvement, and your total lost wages. If you can no longer return to warehouse work, vocational rehabilitation costs may also be included.

There is no "average" settlement, but a crushing injury that requires surgery and results in permanent lift restrictions will be worth significantly more than a minor strain.

Value is driven by:
- The cost of past and future medical treatments or surgeries.
- The severity of your permanent impairment rating from a doctor.
- Your average weekly wage prior to the accident.

Ensure your doctor documents every physical limitation you have. Do not accept a "settlement" offer from an insurance adjuster until you know if you\'ll need future surgery.

A lawyer ensures the insurance company uses the correct wage calculations and doesn\'t lowball your permanent disability rating.
